Our taste receptors only fully taste sugar when it's accompanied by a little salt, so the sweet and salty combination of chips dipped in ice cream is naturally delicious.
correct_answer: Our taste receptors only fully taste sugar when it's accompanied by a little salt, so the sweet and salty combination of chips dipped in ice cream is naturally delicious.

subject: english
skill_name: Evaluate counterclaims
question_text: Consider this claim: People who wake up early are more productive than people who sleep in because waking up early allows for more time to plan and organise the day. correct_answer: Sleeping later helps ensure that people get enough rest; this helps them be more productive because they are sharp and focused throughout the day.

subject: english
skill_name: Evaluate counterclaims
question_text: Consider this claim: The drastic decline of the elk population in Yellowstone National Park in the United States was caused by the reintroduction of wolves, which now kill thousands of elk each year. Now consider how someone might argue against this. Which sentence presents the stronger and more reasonable counter-claim to the above claim?

The reduction of the elk population has been caused by many factors, including other predators, like bears, and drought, which has led to malnutrition and fewer births of calves.
correct_answer: The reduction of the elk population has been caused by many factors, including other predators, like bears, and drought, which has led to malnutrition and fewer births of calves.

subject: english
skill_name: Evaluate counterclaims
question_text: Consider this claim: Parents should be legally allowed to select their baby's eye colour through genetic engineering. Now consider how someone might argue against this. Which sentence presents the stronger and more reasonable counter-claim to the above claim?
options: We can either use genetic engineering to change superficial characteristics like eye colour, or instead we can use it to eliminate the potential for genetically inherited diseases.

Manipulation of human genes is a relatively new science with many unknowns, so it's possible that changing a baby's eye colour could have unintended consequences that ultimately harm the baby's health.
correct_answer: Manipulation of human genes is a relatively new science with many unknowns, so it's possible that changing a baby's eye colour could have unintended consequences that ultimately harm the baby's health.

subject: english
skill_name: Evaluate counterclaims
question_text: Consider this claim: Since it has a level of athleticism similar to that required for traditional Olympic fencing, lightsaber duelling should be made an Olympic sport. Because lightsaber duelling is a new sport, there are not enough experienced competitors from different countries to make it a worthwhile Olympic event.
Lightsabers are made of rigid plastic with LED lights, making them incredibly different from the traditional metal sabres, foils and epees used in fencing.
correct_answer: Option, Allowing a 'sport' based on a film series to become an Olympic event is so frivolous that it would spell the end of the Olympics as we know them.

subject: english
skill_name: Evaluate counterclaims
question_text: Consider this claim: While they both involve jumping and free-falling from up high, skydiving is an overall more thrilling experience than bungee jumping. Because skydivers jump from a plane that is already moving at high speed, they don't experience the rush of acceleration that bungee jumpers feel.
Bungee jumping is an exciting, once-in-a-lifetime thrill ride, but skydiving is a sport that people do over and over again, so it must get boring after a while.
correct_answer: Option, Skydiving has no real 'thrill factor' because jumping out of a plane with both a parachute and a backup parachute is not even slightly risky.

subject: english
skill_name: Evaluate counterclaims
question_text: Consider this claim: Most of today's 'educational' children's television programs are too fast-paced, distracting and fantastical to be considered truly educational. subject: english
skill_name: Evaluate counterclaims

Hot dogs are not a healthy food, but many sandwiches can be healthy choices depending on the bread and fillings used to make them.
correct_answer: A sandwich can be defined as 'two slices of bread or halves of a roll with a filling between them', so a hot dog is a sandwich.

subject: english
skill_name: Evaluate counterclaims

correct_answer: A dog's fear of thunderstorms makes evolutionary sense: over time, dogs may have learned to fear thunderstorms because they can cause dangers like falling tree limbs.

subject: english

subject: english

subject: english
skill_name: Evaluate counterclaims

correct_answer: Walking is more enjoyable than running because it's slower-paced, so walkers can take the time to notice their surroundings and enjoy being outside in nature.

subject: english

skill_name: Evaluate counterclaims

subject: english

options: Because skydivers jump from a plane that is already moving at high speed, they don't experience the rush of acceleration that bungee jumpers feel.
Bungee jumping is an exciting, once-in-a-lifetime thrill ride, but skydiving is a sport that people do over and over again, so it must get boring after a while.

Because lightsaber duelling is a new sport, there are not enough experienced competitors from different countries to make it a worthwhile Olympic event.

skill_name: Choose the analysis that logically connects the evidence to the claim

subject: english
skill_name: Use dictionary entries to determine correct usage
question_text: Look at this dictionary entry. venial vee-nee-yuhl adjective more venial, most venial 1 not serious; minor Forgetting to take out the trash is a venial offense. from Latin (venialis) Which sentence uses the word venial correctly?
options: Although the police officer caught Eva jaywalking, he let her go with only a warning because it was a venial crime.
The newspaper editorial called the mayor venial because she was suspected of taking bribes.
correct_answer: Although the police officer caught Eva jaywalking, he let her go with only a warning because it was a venial crime.

subject: english
skill_name: Use dictionary entries to determine correct usage
question_text: Look at this dictionary entry. regrettable ri-gret-uh-buhl adjective more regrettable, most regrettable 1 causing regret Mrs. Murphy made the regrettable decision to serve the cupcakes before they were fully cooked. from Old French (regreter) Which sentence uses the word regrettable correctly?
options: Contrite and regrettable, the employee apologised to his supervisor for misplacing the expense reports.
The lobbyist's regrettable interruption of the prime minister's speech only damaged the cause he was fighting for.
correct_answer: The lobbyist's regrettable interruption of the prime minister's speech only damaged the cause he was fighting for.

subject: english

options: Chemist Stephanie Kwolek was the brainchild behind Kevlar, the super-strong fabric used in body armour, spacesuits and many other products.
Although Alexander Graham Bell obtained the first patent for a telephone, the device was originally the brainchild of Antonio Meucci.
correct_answer: Although Alexander Graham Bell obtained the first patent for a telephone, the device was originally the brainchild of Antonio Meucci.

subject: english
skill_name: Use dictionary entries to determine correct usage
question_text: Look at this sentence: After the brief recession, stocks were finally starting to rebound. Select the definition of rebound that the sentence uses. rebound \ri-bound\ verb rebounding, rebounded 1. to bounce off of something after impact: The squash ball rebounded off the wall and back into play. 2. to increase or improve after a decline: Mr Thompson's health has rebounded from his week-long illness. 3. (basketball) to catch the ball after a missed shot: Ella rebounded the ball and was able to score. — from Old French (rebondir) Submit
correct_answer: rebound \ri-bound\ verb rebounding, rebounded 1. to bounce off of something after impact: The squash ball rebounded off the wall and back into play. 2. to increase or improve after a decline: Mr Thompson's health has rebounded from his week-long illness. 3. (basketball) to catch the ball after a missed shot: Ella rebounded the ball and was able to score. — from Old French (rebondir)

Select the definition of pathetic that the sentence uses. pathetic \puh-thet-ik\ adjective more pathetic, most pathetic 1. causing feelings of pity: The dogs and cats at the animal shelter were a pathetic sight. 2. inferior or inadequate: That handwritten sign looks pathetic next to the professionally printed one. 3. absurd or laughable: His tasteless jokes were a pathetic attempt to change the subject. — from Greek (pathetikos) Submit

correct_answer: Mia can type using a keyboard with a QWERTY layout, but she prefers the Dvorak layout. The two keyboards have different arrangements of letters and symbols.

subject: english

correct_answer: Sebastian's ill-researched essay about the Space Race received a low mark because it presented such a travesty of the actual historical events.

subject: english

correct_answer: Eva couldn't tolerate the nauseous odour emanating from the landfill, so she rolled up her car windows as she drove past.
